title: Supercruise Exit Types
query: SELECT COUNT(*) AS total FROM SupercruiseExit WHERE event_timestamp >= :startDate AND event_timestamp < :endDate
detailQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN BodyType = 'Station' THEN 'Station' WHEN BodyType = 'Planet' THEN 'Planet' WHEN BodyType = 'PlanetaryRing' THEN 'Planetary Ring' WHEN BodyType = 'Star' THEN 'Star' WHEN BodyType = 'Null' THEN 'Nav Point / Other' ELSE BodyType END AS label, COUNT(*) AS value FROM SupercruiseExit WHERE event_timestamp >= :startDate AND event_timestamp < :endDate GROUP BY label ORDER BY value DESC LIMIT 20

details.text: {total} supercruise exit(s) recorded during the report period. The breakdown by body type tells you what kinds of places you are flying to — frequent station drops mean trading or mission running, planet drops suggest surface exploration or planetary ports, nav points and null-type drops cover megaships, resource extraction sites, and space-based points of interest.
details.help: SupercruiseExit BodyType classifies the destination as Station, Planet, PlanetaryRing, Star, or Null (nav beacons, megaships, combat zones, etc.). total drives the title.

tagLines: Supercruise exit type breakdown compiled from journal records. Destination classification noted.
Stations, planets, nav beacons — the type distribution shows what kind of spacer you are.
Every drop point has a category. The spread reveals your operational habits at a glance.
